message: Workflow failed: Goal Agent exited unsuccessfully: 1

details.output: Committed c9afedb7 (Make Ready Merge own candidate integration). Ready Merge now serializes exact candidate integration and configured-remote push, persists idempotent merge evidence, recovers safely after retry/restart/push failure, then runs optional rebuild on the integrated target and Quality in the round-pinned pre_merge or post_build order. Review approval only accepts the existing integration; decline requires a new auditable round. UI, CLI/API guards, docs, and tests were updated. Exact verification: fresh `cargo test --lib -- --test-threads=1` passed 457/457; `test-full-workflow` passed 1/1; `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, and `git diff --check` passed; the two formerly index-racy workflows passed in the fresh suite plus 30/30 and 20/20 stress reruns. Separate `test-multi-instance-sync` failed 0/1 twice because supervisor-state synchronization deleted the first daemon's new Goal before reconciliation; retained Git history showed that state-sync deletion, outside the Ready Merge integration path.

failed_actions.message: The durable GoalRound foundation model imports and persists tools::host::git_worktrees::MergeResult, coupling Foundation state to a host adapter type.
Ready Merge Git processes lack the workflow execution_id, so workflow cancellation cannot target fetch, merge, or push; cancelled work can continue integrating.
failed_actions.rule: Run long-running Git work through supervised processes with explicit ownership, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: Integration validates node, status, round, and artifact strings but not the active claim, execution, or state revision. Additionally, candidate c9afedb7 is not descended from recorded base 749923d9, so this round is already stale and omits intervening integrated work.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel work through workflow claims, ownership, revisions, and isolated Git branches or worktrees.

failed_actions.message: The concurrency test uses two identical in-process merger calls and does not cover replaced or cancelled claims, unequal revisions, or separate daemons; the reported multi-instance test also failed twice.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to concurrency, persistence, process, Git, and recovery risk.

message: Workflow failed: Four governance rules are violated.

details.output: Committed 6d04fbab (Fence Ready Merge integration across processes) on replayed candidate dcae5e7a from current-main base a413fac2. Moved durable MergeResult evidence into the Foundation Goal model; supervised Ready Merge fetch/merge/push under the exact workflow execution_id with durable cancellation/failure/restart operation evidence; added atomic cross-process claim, execution, node, round, status, candidate, decision-version, and Goal-revision fences before Git side effects and settlement; prevented replaced/cancelled/stale workers from settling; preserved configured target branch/remote and pinned Quality timing; added separate-process cancellation/replacement/unequal-revision/retry/restart/exactly-once coverage; and fixed first-reconciliation state-sync baselines so separate daemons cannot delete unseen remote Goals after a failed sync. Exact verification: cargo test --lib -- --test-threads=1 passed 460/460; cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- test-full-workflow passed 1/1; cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- test-multi-instance-sync passed 4/4 including two-daemon synchronization, cancellation/replacement retry, restart recovery, and child-process production paths; cargo test --lib tools::host::git_sync::tests:: -- --test-threads=1 passed 16/16; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; git diff --check passed; worktree is clean; and a413fac2 is an ancestor of HEAD.

failed_actions.message: Cancellation signals only already-registered subprocesses before marking the claim cancelled, while Git children are spawned before registration. Cancellation can miss a newly launched Git process, and the signalled path can leave workflow capacity unreleased.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: Ready Merge drops the coordination lock between fence verification and each Git side effect or evidence write. The existing-integration path also returns without a final fence, and operation success plus later workflow transitions occur after the last check, so a cancelled or replaced execution can still integrate or settle.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The 4/4 multi-process gate passes, but cancellation is tested only after the push process is visible; it does not cover launch or post-fence settlement races. Unequal revisions are covered only by a single-process unit test that directly inserts a claim, not by the required separate-process production path.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.
